  "text": "The inter-broker replication protocol in apache/kafka is implemented across both Scala and Java source files (all compiled to JVM bytecode).\n\n**ReplicaFetcherThread**: The primary implementation is in `core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaFetcherThread.scala`, which extends `AbstractFetcherThread` (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/AbstractFetcherThread.scala`). The abstract manager `AbstractFetcherManager` and its concrete subclass `ReplicaFetcherManager` (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaFetcherManager.scala`) manage the lifecycle of fetcher threads. Two endpoint implementations exist: `LocalLeaderEndPoint` and `RemoteLeaderEndPoint` (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/`). The Java interface `LeaderEndPoint` (`server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/LeaderEndPoint.java`) defines the contract. Supporting Java types include `PartitionFetchState`, `ReplicaFetch`, and `ReplicaState` in the server module. Additionally, `ReplicaAlterLogDirsThread` handles cross-dir replication, and `TierStateMachine` (`core/src/main/java/kafka/server/TierStateMachine.java`) manages remote-tiered storage fetching state.\n\n**LeaderEpochFileCache**: Implemented in `storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/epoch/LeaderEpochFileCache.java`. The persistent backing is `LeaderEpochCheckpointFile` (`storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/checkpoint/LeaderEpochCheckpointFile.java`). These are used by followers to detect log divergence and determine the correct truncation point.\n\n**LogStartOffset tracking**: `UnifiedLog` (`storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/UnifiedLog.java`) owns the `logStartOffset` field and `maybeIncrementLogStartOffset()` method. The reasons for incrementing are enumerated in `LogStartOffsetIncrementReason` (`storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/LogStartOffsetIncrementReason.java`). The `replica.ReplicaState` record (`server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/replica/ReplicaState.java`) tracks `logStartOffset` per remote replica. `LogLoader` (`storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/LogLoader.java`) recovers the log start offset on startup.\n\n**ISR shrink/expand**: The partition-level ISR management is in `Partition.scala` (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/cluster/Partition.scala`) via `maybeShrinkIsr()` and `maybeExpandIsr()`. These trigger `AlterPartitionManager` (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/AlterPartitionManager.scala`) to send `AlterPartitionRequest`/`AlterPartitionResponse` to the controller. On the controller side, `ReplicationControlManager` (`metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/controller/ReplicationControlManager.java`) processes ISR changes, backed by `BrokersToIsrs` (ISR index), `PartitionChangeBuilder` (builds change records), `LeaderAndIsr`, and `PartitionRegistration` in the metadata module. `ReplicaManager` (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaManager.scala`) orchestrates high-watermark advancement and ISR expansion checks."
}

## Inter-Broker Replication Protocol in apache/kafka

**ReplicaFetcherThread** (core fetching mechanism):
- `core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaFetcherThread.scala` — main implementation
- `core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/AbstractFetcherThread.scala` — base class with fetch loop
- `core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaFetcherManager.scala` + `AbstractFetcherManager.scala` — thread lifecycle
- `core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/LocalLeaderEndPoint.scala` / `RemoteLeaderEndPoint.scala` — fetch endpoints
- `server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/LeaderEndPoint.java` — Java interface
- `server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/PartitionFetchState.java` / `ReplicaFetch.java` — state types
- `core/src/main/java/kafka/server/TierStateMachine.java` — remote tier fetch state machine

**LeaderEpochFileCache**:
- `storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/epoch/LeaderEpochFileCache.java` — main implementation
- `storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/checkpoint/LeaderEpochCheckpointFile.java` — persistence layer

**LogStartOffset tracking**:
- `storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/UnifiedLog.java` — owns `logStartOffset`, `maybeIncrementLogStartOffset()`
- `storage/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/storage/internals/log/LogStartOffsetIncrementReason.java` — increment reasons enum
- `server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/replica/ReplicaState.java` — per-replica `logStartOffset` tracking

**ISR shrink/expand**:
- `core/src/main/scala/kafka/cluster/Partition.scala` — `maybeShrinkIsr()` / `maybeExpandIsr()`
- `core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/AlterPartitionManager.scala` — sends AlterPartition to controller
- `metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/controller/ReplicationControlManager.java` — controller-side ISR processing
- `metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/controller/BrokersToIsrs.java` — ISR index
- `metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/controller/PartitionChangeBuilder.java` — builds ISR change records
- `metadata/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/metadata/LeaderAndIsr.java` — ISR state data

package org.apache.kafka.server;

import org.apache.kafka.common.Uuid;
import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;

import java.util.Optional;

/**
 * Class to keep partition offset and its state (truncatingLog, delayed)

  "text": "The inter-broker replication protocol in apache/kafka is implemented across both Scala and Java source files (all compiled to JVM bytecode).\n\n**ReplicaFetcherThread**: The primary implementation is in `core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/ReplicaFetcherThread.scala`, which extends `AbstractFetcherThread` (`core/src/main/scala/kafka/server/AbstractFetcherThread.scala`).
